title: Work with us
intro: Peninsula Youth Theatre is built by a small staff and a generous community of volunteers. Whether you're looking for a paid role on our team or a way to help us put on a show, we'd love to hear from you.
roles_intro: Open paid positions. We post new roles here as they become available. If you don't see a fit today, check back — or send us a note and we'll keep your resume on file.

roles.title: Production Manager
roles.description: Lead the logistics, scheduling, and technical needs of our three Mainstage productions per year. Work closely with directors, designers, and youth performers to keep every production running on time and on budget.

roles.title: Teaching Artist (Drama)
roles.description: Lead after-school drama classes and weekend workshops for students ages 6–14. Looking for working theatre professionals with experience teaching young people. Roughly 6–10 hours per week, with flexible scheduling.

volunteer_intro: Volunteers are essential to everything we do. Whether you have a few hours during a show weekend or want a regular role behind the scenes, there's a way to help.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Front-of-house volunteer
volunteer_opportunities.time_commitment: About 4 hours per show, sign up for as many or as few as you like
volunteer_opportunities.description: Greet families, scan tickets, help with concessions, and answer questions during our Mainstage productions. The best way to see our shows for free, and a great way to support PYT a few times a year.

volunteer_opportunities.title: Costume shop helper
volunteer_opportunities.description: Help our costume designer prep, mend, organize, and pull costumes for upcoming productions. Sewing skills welcome but not required — we have plenty of tasks for every skill level. A great fit for parents whose kids are in our programs.
